description:
This image is a screenshot of a webpage promoting an AI-powered CV creation service. The main text, written in French, reads: "Créez un CV qui vous fait embaucher — Propulsé par l'IA," which translates to "Create a CV that gets you hired — Powered by AI." Above the main text, there is a smaller banner with the text "Gratuit • Alimenté par l'IA," meaning "Free • Powered by AI." Below the main text, there is a brief description stating that users can create a professional and multilingual CV in a few minutes, optimized for their dream job.

There are two buttons below the description: one blue button labeled "Commencer gratuitement" (Start for free) and another outlined button labeled "En savoir plus" (Learn more). To the right of the text, there is an illustration of a CV template with a blue header and placeholder text lines.

The background is white with black sidebars on the left and right edges. In the top right corner, there is a menu icon.

Filename: AI_Powered_CV_Creator_French_Webpage_Screenshot

This image is a screenshot of a campaign webpage for Benjamin Rankin, a candidate for the Parti Vert (Green Party). The top section features the Parti Vert logo and name in white text on a dark green background, along with Benjamin Rankin's name. There are social media icons for Facebook and Instagram, as well as a button labeled "S'impliquer" (Get Involved).

The main content area has a bright green background. On the left side, there is text in French that reads: "Élection partielle | 13 avril 2026" (By-election | April 13, 2026) and "Votez pour du Changement dans Terrebonne" (Vote for Change in Terrebonne). The word "Changement" is highlighted in yellow. Below this, there is a description stating that Benjamin Rankin is a community leader ready to fight for affordable housing, health, education, and the environment. A button labeled "Mes priorités" (My priorities) is also present.

On the right side, there is a circular photo of Benjamin Rankin, who is smiling and wearing a maroon hoodie. His name is written below the photo in large white letters.

At the bottom of the image, there is a yellow section with the text "Faites passer le message!" (Spread the message!) in black.

Filename: Benjamin_Rankin_Parti_Vert_Campaign_Page.png

This image is a screenshot from the website "Cohabitat.cc," showcasing a project focused on sustainable urban living. The left side of the image features text in French that translates to "Sustainable Design." It describes an urban sanctuary for builders of a future without cars, emphasizing architecture designed for relational richness rather than material accumulation. Below the text, there are two buttons: one labeled "Discover the model" with a download icon, and another labeled "Plan."

The right side of the image displays a photograph of a modern building with large glass windows and a sign that reads "COHABITAT." The building has a green wall and a balcony with string lights. There are people visible inside the building, which appears to house a restaurant or café. A cyclist is seen passing by in front of the building. A caption below the photo indicates that the proposed project is located in Bellechasse, near the Rosemont metro station, with an expected delivery date of summer 2029.

Filename: Cohabitat_Sustainable_Urban_Design_Project.jpg

This image shows a user interface for managing CVs (curriculum vitae) on a website or application. The page is titled "Liste des CVs" which translates to "List of CVs" in English. Below the title, there is a brief description stating that users can create new CVs, modify existing ones, or export them as PDFs.

The main section of the page displays a table with columns labeled "Langue" (Language), "Titre" (Title), "Dernière mise à jour" (Last Updated), "Statut" (Status), "Description du poste" (Job Description), and "Actions". 

There is one entry in the table:
- Language: Français (French)
- Title: Profil entrepreneur (Entrepreneur Profile)
- Last Updated: 21 avr. 2026 (April 21, 2026)
- Status: En traitement (In progress)
- Job Description: Aucune description de poste (No job description)

Under the "Actions" column, there are three icons: an eye icon (likely for viewing), a pencil icon (likely for editing), and a downward arrow icon (likely for downloading).

At the top left corner, there is a logo resembling a green paperclip, and at the top right corner, there is a menu icon.

Filename: "CV_List_French_Interface.png"

This image is a screenshot of a webpage from "Projet Montréal," featuring Elvira Carhuallanqui, the city councillor for Parc-Extension. The page has a green background with white text and is primarily in French. 

At the top left, there is a circular profile picture of Elvira Carhuallanqui, accompanied by her name and title. Below her name, there are language options including Français, English, Español, Ελληνικά, ਪੰਜਾਬੀ, বাংলা, اردو, and हिन्दी, with "Français" highlighted.

The main heading reads "Votre pouvoir d'agir sur Parc-Extension," which translates to "Your power to act on Parc-Extension." It emphasizes the importance of reporting issues using the 311 service to maintain a clean and safe environment.

Below the heading, there are two sections:
1. **Application 311**: Describes the app as the fastest way to report issues like potholes, graffiti, and broken streetlights, with a button labeled "Télécharger l'app" (Download the app).
2. **Ligne 311**: Encourages calling an agent 7 days a week for any questions about municipal services, with a prompt to dial 311.

Further down, there is a list of issues people can intervene on, such as uncollected garbage, damaged sidewalks or roads, defective street lighting, snow removal problems, and vandalized urban furniture.

Filename: `Elvira_Carhuallanqui_Parc_Extension_311_Service_Info_Fr.png`

This image is a screenshot from the website "Cohabitat.cc," displaying financial details and operational costs for a project. The layout is divided into several sections:

1. **Projet Global (Mensuel)**: This section, highlighted in black, shows monthly financial projections:
   - Hypothèques: $33,630.81
   - Obligations Comm.: $16,355.99
   - OPEX Total: $51,437.10
   - Revenus (Comm. + Flottantes): $21,859.08
   - Solde Net Mensuel: $79,564.82

2. **Détail des Frais d'Opération (Mensuel)**: This section lists monthly operational expenses:
   - Services & Gestion: Maintenance & Nettoyage ($2,500), Gestion Administrative ($1,000), Internet & Tech ($250), Vérification Comptable ($300)
   - Énergie & Chauffage: $4,180.65
   - Provision Amortissement: $3,718.25

3. **Loyers Fondateurs (Par Suite)**: This orange-highlighted section provides details on founder rents per suite:
   - Rent: $2,652.16/month
   - Impact Apport Initial: Remboursement obligations (-$1,363)
   - Coût net réel: $1,289.16

4. **Apport Par Fondateur (12)**: This section outlines the total capital investment by founders:
   - Total Capital injecté de départ: $206,528.90
   - Mise de Fonds Hypothécaire: $174,112.23
   - Frais de Lancement: $32,416.67

The image also includes a sidebar with social media icons and a menu button at the top right corner.

**Filename**: Cohabitat_Financial_Operational_Details_Screenshot

This image is a screenshot of a website for an initiative called "Neuf." The top section features an aerial photograph of a neighborhood with houses, roads, and green spaces. Overlaid on this image is text in French that reads: "Neuf : Votre quartier prend vie ! Ensemble, on renouvelle notre quartier," which translates to "Neuf: Your neighborhood comes to life! Together, we renew our neighborhood."

Below the image, there is a navigation menu with options labeled "À propos," "Actions," "Engager," and "Contactez-nous." The main content area welcomes visitors to the Neuf site, describing it as a citizen initiative focused on cleanliness and renewal of the neighborhood. It emphasizes individual responsibility and collective action to improve the environment.

The "Notre mission" (Our mission) section explains that the Neuf initiative aims to promote a culture of cleanliness through individual actions and create a ripple effect for collective environmental stewardship.

At the bottom, there is a footer with copyright information for 2025, contact details (an email address), and a link to follow the initiative on Facebook.

Filename: Neuf_Initiative_Website_Screenshot.jpg

This image is a screenshot of a webpage from the Parti Vert (Green Party) website, featuring Benjamin Rankin. The top section has a dark green background with navigation links on the left side, including "Accueil," "À propos," "Priorités," "Thèmes," "Pourquoi Vert ?," "Rejoignez," and "Terrebonne." There is also a white button labeled "S'impliquer" (Get Involved) on the right side.

Below the navigation menu, there is a white section with four illustrated cards promoting different campaign messages:
1. "Votez pour PLUS DE SOINS DE SANTÉ" (Vote for MORE HEALTHCARE), with an illustration of hands holding a tree with colorful fruits.
2. "Votez pour PAIX ET SÉCURITÉ" (Vote for PEACE AND SECURITY), with an illustration of people hugging each other.
3. "Votez pour METTRE FIN À LA HAINE" (Vote for ENDING HATE), with an illustration of hands holding megaphones.
4. "Votez pour RÉCONCILIATION AUTOCHTONE" (Vote for INDIGENOUS RECONCILIATION), with an illustration of hands forming a circle around a sun.

A "Mettre en pause" (Pause) button is located above these cards.

Filename: `parti_vert_campaign_screenshot.png`

This image is a screenshot of a website for "Resto Danny," which appears to be dedicated to the legacy of Danny Ng and his contributions to the community. The top section of the image features a navigation bar with links labeled "L'Histoire," "Le Fonds," "Le Projet," "Le Modèle," and a button that says "Nous soutenir." The main content area has a large, bold title in French: "Héritage Danny Ng : Nourrir la communauté," which translates to "Danny Ng Heritage: Feeding the Community." Below the title, there is a subtitle that reads, "De la passion d'un homme à une mission durable pour Montréal," meaning "From a man's passion to a sustainable mission for Montreal."

The text explains that the site is dedicated to the memory of Danny (Wing Chai) Ng and the continuation of his work. It mentions transforming his heritage into concrete action for food security in the Villeray-Saint-Michel-Parc-Extension district and throughout Montreal. At the bottom of the text, there is an orange button with the text "Soutenir le projet," which means "Support the project."

The background of the image shows an aerial view of a residential area with houses, streets, and green spaces, likely representing the community mentioned in the text.

Filename: RestoDanny_HeritageWebsite_Screenshot.jpg
